label: South Africa
countryDocumentSourceName: South African Department of Home Affairs
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Common source for passports and civil records.
typicalArrivalNotes: Long-haul movers often benefit from stronger pre-arrival planning.
Civil record replacement and legalisation review should not be left to the last minute.

label: India
countryDocumentSourceName: Ministry of External Affairs / local authorities
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Common source for passports, attestation and document services.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document attestation and legalisation often take longer; plan early.
Education and civil records may need extra processing for use in the Netherlands.

label: United States
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Passports and apostille services vary by state.
typicalArrivalNotes: Apostille is often used for US documents; check state-level requirements.
Long-distance moves benefit from advance planning for shipping and first-week setup.

label: United Kingdom
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Official guidance on birth, marriage and legalisation services.
typicalArrivalNotes: Post-Brexit document validation may differ from EU routes; check current requirements.
Driving licence exchange rules for UK licence holders are set by RDW.

label: Canada
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and authentication services vary by province.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document authentication for use abroad often involves provincial and federal steps.
Long-haul logistics and temporary housing planning are often useful.

label: Australia
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Passports, birth certificates and apostille/authentication services.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document legalisation for the Netherlands may involve DFAT authentication.
Long-distance moves often need extra buffer for shipping and first-month admin.

label: Germany
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from German authorities.
typicalArrivalNotes: EU/EEA moves often have simpler document and driving licence pathways.
Registration and BSN sequencing still matter for a smooth start.

label: France
countryDocumentSourceName: Mairie / French civil and consular services
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from French authorities.
typicalArrivalNotes: EU moves typically have lower document friction; focus on registration and first-week sequencing.
Driving licence use and exchange are covered by RDW and EU rules.

label: Spain
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and official documents from Spanish authorities.
typicalArrivalNotes: EU/EEA document and mobility pathways usually apply.
Municipality registration and BSN remain the main early priorities.

label: Brazil
countryDocumentSourceSummary: Civil records and document legalisation via cartórios and consulates.
typicalArrivalNotes: Document legalisation for use in the Netherlands can take time; start early.
Long-haul moves often benefit from shipping and temporary housing planning.
